title: pyrus venus
side_effects: Pyrus venus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches, which may occur due to overconsumption or incorrect preparation of the plant. In severe cases, excessive use of Pyrus venus can lead to more serious complications, including allergic reactions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. Some individuals may also experience skin irritation or rashes after coming into contact with the plant's sap or oils. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also have long-term effects on liver and kidney function.
